content:
- [project_architecture.md](project_architecture.md) — Single-file HTML/React RPG, ~7100 lines. CLAUDE.md has full map.
- [project_workflow.md](project_workflow.md) — Source of truth and update workflow (md first, then HTML)
- [project_scene_overhaul.md](project_scene_overhaul.md) — Major scene restructuring: 70 scene min, hub system, broken fixes, final act rework
- [project_combat_narrator.md](project_combat_narrator.md) — Dynamic DM narrator for combat, future equipment degradation & scars
-

- [feedback_token_efficiency.md](feedback_token_efficiency.md) — Use CLAUDE.md map, avoid redundant exploration, minimize token usage
- [feedback_scene_transitions.md](feedback_scene_transitions.md) — Write hub/transition scenes with full prose, not just menus
- [feedback_version_bump.md](feedback_version_bump.md) — Always bump version number before every push
- [feedback_versioning.md](feedback_versioning.md) — Version scheme: major.medium.small (X.x.x)
- [feedback_loading_screen.md](feedback_l
- [reference_qmd.md](reference_qmd.md) — qmd tool installed for token-efficient file access

name: Dynamic version numbers
description: All version displays must read from CAMPAIGN data, never hardcoded. Only bump campaign.json + theme.json.
type: feedback
---

Version numbers must never be hardcoded in the engine. The char sheet header and any other display reads from `CAMPAIGN?.version` dynamically.

**Why:** User found a hardcoded "v19" in the character sheet long after the version had moved to v24+. Hardcoded versions rot silently.

**How to apply:** On every version bump, only two

name: keep-loading-screen-updated
description: Always update the loading screen stats (version, scene count) when pushing changes
type: feedback
---

Always update the loading screen (`#loading-sub` around line 952) with the current version and scene count before pushing. It shows version, scene count, and companion count.

**Why:** User wants the loading splash to reflect accurate stats so playtesters see correct info immediately.

**How to apply:** When bumping version or adding/removing scene

name: natural-scene-transitions
description: User prioritizes natural flow between scenes over mechanical routing fixes
type: feedback
---

When restructuring scenes, focus on making transitions feel natural for the player. More scenes will be added to smooth transitions.

**Why:** The user sees this as a storytelling project, not just a graph-fixing exercise. Mechanical "hub routing" scenes need atmosphere and narrative purpose.

**How to apply:** When creating hub scenes or transition scenes,

name: feedback_token_efficiency
description: User wants minimal token usage — use CLAUDE.md map, avoid redundant exploration, use targeted reads
type: feedback
---

Minimize token usage in sessions by using CLAUDE.md architectural map instead of re-exploring the codebase.

**Why:** User explicitly asked to reduce Claude Code token costs.
**How to apply:**
- Read CLAUDE.md first for line ranges instead of grepping/globbing for known structures
- Use targeted Read with offset/limit instead of read

name: always-bump-version
description: Always update the version number on the title screen when pushing changes
type: feedback
---

Always bump the version number in the CoverScreen footer before every push to GitHub. The version string is in the HTML at the bottom of the CoverScreen component (search for `'v19.`). Also copy to index.html after updating.

**Why:** User wants the deployed version to always reflect the latest changes so playtesters can verify they have the current build.

**How t

name: versioning-scheme
description: Version numbering convention - major.medium.small changes
type: feedback
---

Use semantic-style versioning: vX.x.x where:
- **X** (major): Large changes (full restructuring, new systems, major overhauls)
- **x** (medium): Medium changes (routing fixes, new scenes, UI improvements)
- **x** (small): Small changes (bug fixes, text tweaks, version bumps)

**Why:** User wants version numbers to meaningfully reflect the scope of changes.

**How to apply:** Judge t

name: project_architecture
description: Knights Oath is a single-file HTML/React RPG (~7100 lines). No build system. Key sections and line ranges documented in CLAUDE.md.
type: project
---

Single-file browser RPG: index.html (~7100 lines), React createElement (no JSX), inline CSS+JS.
D&D 5e 2024 rules, Dragonlance setting. 220+ scenes, 9 companions, 15 combat encounters.

**Why:** Understanding the architecture prevents expensive re-exploration each session.
**How to apply:** Read CLAUDE.md at

name: scene-overhaul-plan
description: Major scene restructuring initiative - 70 scene minimum, hub system, broken scene fixes, final act rework
type: project
---

User wants a major overhaul of the scene flow in Knights Oath v19:

1. **Fix broken scenes first** — Scenes 44, 26, 28, 31 don't exist but are referenced. Scene 44 is critical (field exercise dead-end).
2. **Split Scene 11 into time-gated hubs** — Morning/Afternoon/Evening hubs with minimum-visit gates to prevent 90% content bypass.
3

name: Task Backlog
description: Prioritized list of features and fixes for Knight's Oath - reference when user asks "what's next?"
type: project
---

- [x] **4. Affection bar** — Show companion approval bar in CharSheet (v24.5.24)
- [x] **16. Mira healing priority** — Heals lowest HP% first, ties: PC > Mira > others (v24.5.24)
- [x] **17. HP persistence** — Already working correctly (long rest/level up only)
- [x] **Title system** — Earn/select titles, persist across campaigns (v24.5.19)
- [x] **Campaign import/export** — JSON file download/upload + localStorage (v24.5.22)
- [x] **New Game+** — Restart campaign with leveled character in stand

- [ ] **3. Survival mechanics** — Waterskin uses, rations consumption, foraging/hunting during rests (Survival checks)
- [ ] **15. Kill tracking** — Track who kills what for weapon stuck mechanic and story flags
- [ ] **5. Response scenes** — Should show NPC response to action before returning to hub; audit scenes that skip this

- User wants to discuss each large item before coding
- Survival system: discuss how punishing (optional hardcore mode vs always on?)
- Combat reactions: Shield spell is reaction (not Shield of Faith which is bonus action)

name: knights-oath-workflow
description: Source of truth and update workflow for the Knights Oath interactive fiction project
type: project
---

Source of truth for the story is `Book1_Knights_Oath_Prelude_v19_COMPLETE.md`. Changes go there first, then get propagated to `Knights_Oath_Interactive_v19.html`.

**Why:** The markdown file is the canonical story content; the HTML is the interactive build derived from it.

**How to apply:** Always edit the markdown first, then update the HTML to match.

name: qmd-tool
description: User has qmd installed for token-efficient file access in the knights-oath namespace
type: reference
---

`qmd` is installed and used to save tokens when accessing project files. Namespace: `knights-oath`. Use `qmd ls knights-oath` to list files and qmd commands to read content instead of reading large files directly.
